decisions.decision: Keep the step backend-only and route-level: wire `/v1/health` inside `backend/server.py` to the existing `v1_health_payload()` helper, while leaving `/health` and `/healthz` on the legacy `health_payload()` contract.
alternatives_rejected: Rebuilding the versioned health payload inline inside `route_request()`
Changing the frontend health client or the HTTP handler dispatch for this step

decisions.decision: Treat `/v1/health` like the other explicit `/v1/*` resources: `GET` returns the versioned health payload and any other method on the same path returns `405 method_not_allowed`, while legacy probe paths keep their existing GET-only special case.
alternatives_rejected: Letting non-`GET` `/v1/health` requests fall through to the generic router `404 not_found`
Upgrading `/health` and `/healthz` to the versioned error and payload contract

decisions.decision: Advertise the new endpoint from the root service index as the bare path `/v1/health`, appended after the existing legacy probes so `routes.health` becomes [`/health`, `/healthz`, `/v1/health`].
alternatives_rejected: Replacing or reordering the legacy probe entries

steps.task: Update `backend/server.py` so `service_index_payload()` advertises `/v1/health` alongside `/health` and `/healthz`, preserving the existing bare-path style and legacy ordering for the health route list.

Extend `route_request()` in `backend/server.py` with an explicit `/v1/health` resource branch that returns `v1_health_payload()` for `GET` and raises the standard `405 method_not_allowed` error for other methods, without changing the legacy `/health` and `/healthz` handlers.

Add targeted assertions in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` covering the updated root service index health list, successful `GET /v1/health` routing, `405` behavior for non-`GET` `/v1/health` requests, and continued legacy payload compatibility for `/health` and `/healthz`.
Run the focused backend API tests for the root index and health routes, then review the diff and commit the implementation once the targeted coverage passes.
